examTip: This topic appears in almost every WB Police SI exam — 5 to 8 questions guaranteed. Focus on Article numbers.

sections.heading: 🔑 Must-Know Article Numbers
points: Art 12–35 = Fundamental Rights (Part III)
Art 36–51 = Directive Principles of State Policy (Part IV)
Art 51A = Fundamental Duties (added by 42nd Amendment 1976)
Art 21A = Right to Education (added by 86th Amendment 2002) — Free education 6–14 years
Art 32 = Right to Constitutional Remedies — 'Heart & Soul of Constitution' (Dr. Ambedkar)
Art 17 = Abolition of Untouchability
Art 44 = Uniform Civil Code (DPSP)
Art 356 = President's Rule in states
Art 368 = Amendment of Constitution
Art 370 = (Was) Special Status to J&K — Abrogated August 2019
Art 123 = President's Ordinance Power
Art 124 = Establishment of Supreme Court
Art 214 = Establishment of High Courts
Art 243 = Panchayati Raj (73rd Amendment 1992)
Art 324 = Election Commission of India

sections.heading: 📅 Critical Amendments — Exam Favourites
points: 1st Amendment (1951): Added 9th Schedule (land reform laws, judicial review protected)
42nd Amendment (1976): Added Socialist, Secular, Integrity to Preamble; added Fundamental Duties
44th Amendment (1978): Removed Right to Property from FRs (now Art 300A — legal right only)
52nd Amendment (1985): Anti-defection law (10th Schedule)
61st Amendment (1988): Voting age 21 → 18
73rd Amendment (1992): Panchayati Raj constitutionalized
74th Amendment (1992): Municipalities constitutionalized
86th Amendment (2002): Art 21A — Right to Education
101st Amendment (2016): GST introduced
103rd Amendment (2019): 10% EWS reservation

sections.heading: ⚠️ Exam Traps — Common Wrong Answers
points: Governor is appointed by PRESIDENT — NOT by the state legislature or PM directly
Rajya Sabha is PERMANENT — cannot be dissolved. Only Lok Sabha can be dissolved.
Constitution was ADOPTED on 26 Nov 1949 but came into FORCE on 26 Jan 1950
India is a Republic since 26 Jan 1950 — NOT since 15 Aug 1947 (that was Independence)
Right to Property is NO LONGER a Fundamental Right — it's a Constitutional Right (Art 300A)
DPSP are NON-JUSTICIABLE — you cannot go to court to enforce them
Fundamental Duties are also NON-JUSTICIABLE but courts can use them for interpretation

sections.heading: 🏛️ Parliament — Quick Numbers
points: Lok Sabha: max 552 members (530 states + 20 UTs + 2 Anglo-Indian nominated — nomination removed now)
Rajya Sabha: max 250 members (238 elected + 12 nominated by President)
Current Lok Sabha: 543 elected seats
Lok Sabha term: 5 years (can be dissolved early)
Rajya Sabha: No dissolution, 1/3 retire every 2 years, each member serves 6 years
Money Bill: Can only be introduced in Lok Sabha, Rajya Sabha has 14 days only
Joint Sitting: Presided by Speaker of Lok Sabha
